diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Diffuse large B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma with erythema nodosum
diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Dermal lymphomatous deposits, granulomatous diseases (tuberculosis, histoplasmosis, sarcoidosis)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with widespread lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly, was diagnosed with stage IIIB diffuse large B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma, and developed erythematous skin nodules consistent with erythema nodosum after chemotherapy. Histological examination of biopsied nodules confirmed erythema nodosum without evidence of lymphomatous infiltration. The normalization of FDG uptake in previously positive areas of lymphoma and the response of erythema nodosum to anti-inflammatory treatment supported the final diagnosis.
